sections.paragraph:
θέαμα, ατος, τό (s. next entry; Semonides, Fgm. 7, 67; Aeschyl.; Strabo 14, 2, 5 of the seven ‘wonders’ of the world; CPJ 519, 60 [II/III a.d.]; Ex 3:3 Sym.; Philo; Joseph.; Mel., P. 19, 127; 29, 197) someth. notable exhibited to view, a sight, spectacle GJs 19:2f.—DELG s.v. θέα.

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

CPJ CPJ = Corpus Papyrorum Judaicarum—List 4

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 445
